name: Sauteed Shrimp and Rice with Fresh Corn
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Fresh corn kernels (from 2 Ears of corn)
8 ounces Medium-sized shrimp, shelled and deveined
1/2 teaspoon Fresh thyme leaves, stripped from stems
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the green tops from the scallions and thinly slice them. Set aside.
2. Chop the white and pale-green parts of the scallions.
3. Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a medium-sized saucepan.
4. Add the chopped scallion to the pan and sauté until tender, about 2 minutes.
5. Stir in the rice and sauté for an additional 2 minutes.
6. Add water, wine, and salt. Bring to a boil, stirring well.
7. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 12 minutes.
8. Uncover and stir in corn, shrimp, remaining tablespoon of butter, basil, and thyme.
9. Cover and cook until the liquid is absorbed and shrimp are cooked, about 3 minutes.
10. Sprinkle with green scallion tops and garnish with lemon wedges before serving. Leftovers can be served cold.
